# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data (phase 1: fast startup; no encryption/backup policies).

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - m5.large
      - m6i.large
      - c6i.large
  LatestAmiId:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-6.1-x86_64
  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
  DataVolumeSizeGiB:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 10
    MaxValue: 16384
  DataVolumeType:
    Type: String
    Default: gp3
    AllowedValues:
      - gp3
      - gp2
      - io1
      - io2
      - st1
      - sc1
  DataDeviceName:
    Type: String
    Default: /dev/sdf
  AllowSshCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
  AppPort:
    Type: Number
    Default: 8080
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 65535
  AllowAppCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0

Conditions:
  UseMultipleInstances: !Not [!Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 1]]

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: App instance security group (SSH + app port)
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowSshCidr
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: !Ref AppPort
          ToPort: !Ref AppPort
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowAppCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-sg

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: AttachDetachEbsForStackInstances
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:AttachVolume
                  - ec2:DetachVolume
                  - ec2:DescribeVolumes
                  - ec2:DescribeInstances
                  - ec2:DescribeTags
                Resource: '*'

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  AppLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt InstanceProfile.Arn
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !GetAtt InstanceSecurityGroup.GroupId
        MetadataOptions:
          HttpTokens: required
          HttpEndpoint: enabled
        BlockDeviceMappings:
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: 20
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: true
        TagSpecifications:
          - ResourceType: instance
            Tags:
              - Key: Name
                Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-app
          - ResourceType: volume
            Tags:
              - Key: Name
                Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-root
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash
            set -euxo pipefail

            dnf -y update || true
            dnf -y install amazon-ssm-agent cloud-utils-growpart || true
            systemctl enable --now amazon-ssm-agent || true

            # Wait for the data device to appear
            DATA_DEV="${DataDeviceName}"
            for i in $(seq 1 60); do
              if [ -b "$DATA_DEV" ]; then
                break
              fi
              sleep 2
            done

            # Format and mount if present
            if [ -b "$DATA_DEV" ]; then
              mkdir -p /data
              if ! blkid "$DATA_DEV" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                mkfs.xfs -f "$DATA_DEV"
              fi
              UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$DATA_DEV")
              grep -q "$UUID" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=$UUID /data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
              mount -a
              chown -R ec2-user:ec2-user /data || true
            fi

  AppAsg:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref SubnetId
      MinSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      MaxSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      DesiredCapacity: !Ref InstanceCount
      HealthCheckType: EC2
      HealthCheckGracePeriod: 60
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref AppLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt AppLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-app
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

  DataVolumeA:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSizeGiB
      VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-data-a

  DataVolumeB:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSizeGiB
      VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-data-b

  DataVolumeC:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSizeGiB
      VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-data-c

  DataVolumeD:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSizeGiB
      VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-data-d

  AttachDataVolumeA:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Select
        - 0
        - !GetAtt AppAsg.Instances
      VolumeId: !Ref DataVolumeA
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName

  AttachDataVolumeB:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Select
        - 1
        - !GetAtt AppAsg.Instances
      VolumeId: !Ref DataVolumeB
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName

  AttachDataVolumeC:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Select
        - 2
        - !GetAtt AppAsg.Instances
      VolumeId: !Ref DataVolumeC
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName

  AttachDataVolumeD:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Select
        - 3
        - !GetAtt AppAsg.Instances
      VolumeId: !Ref DataVolumeD
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Value: !GetAtt InstanceSecurityGroup.GroupId
  AutoScalingGroupName:
    Value: !Ref AppAsg
  DataMountPath:
    Value: /data
  DataDeviceName:
    Value: !Ref DataDeviceName
  DataVolumeAId:
    Value: !Ref DataVolumeA
  DataVolumeBId:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Value: !Ref DataVolumeB
  DataVolumeCId:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Value: !Ref DataVolumeC
  DataVolumeDId:
    Condition: UseMultipleInstances
    Value: !Ref DataVolumeD
